free_text: Search for nondirect effects in the 28Si(p,p0,1,2)28Si
excitation function from 16-18.2 MeV
The targets were made by breaking a bubble blown from
a quartz tube and were 20 keV thick to 17-MeV protons.
The 2000-mu-m Si surface-barrier detectors
cooled to -10 degrees C.
OG
To determine the reliability of the data, two checks
were made. The first was by measurement of the
elastic scattering of protons from gold evaporated on
the Si target simultaneously with the 28Si(p,p)28Si
data. The extracted Au(p,p) yields were compared with
optical-model calculations using the universal
optical parameters of Becchetti and Greenlees from
J,PR,182,1190,1969. Agreement was within +/-5% over the
entire energy range. The second consistency check was a
comparison of the 16O(p,p)16O yields with those
measured by Daehnick from J,PR,135,B1168,1964. The
agreement was +/-8% for all three counters over the
entire energy range.
A combination of the statistical error and
the over-all repeatability of the values obtained in
the three separate runs made.
Figs.1-3 in Phys.Rev.C,5,1257,1972 was
digitized by GSYS2.4.7.
